article.title: DEA reminder: 90-day Schedule II prescription supply limits remain in effect
article.summary: DEA reminded prescribers that Schedule II prescriptions remain limited to a 90-day supply per 21 CFR 1306.12, with multiple sequential prescriptions permitted only when patient diagnosis and clinical need are documented. State law may impose stricter limits.
article.rawContent: DEA's notice reaffirms longstanding policy under 21 CFR 1306.12 governing Schedule II prescriptions: (1) no refills may be authorized; (2) a practitioner may issue up to three sequential prescriptions for the same drug to a single patient, each for up to a 30-day supply, allowing up to a 90-day total; (3) the practitioner must include the earliest fill date on each prescription beyond the first; (4) state laws may impose tighter limits — e.g., 7-day initial limits for opioids in NY, FL, and several other states. Prescriptions written outside these parameters may be invalid and subject practitioners to administrative or civil action.
